summary: Matched several previously stubbed functions in src/melee/gm/gm_1832.c, centered on demo/game-mode enter and rendering callbacks. The PR added typed static state for lbl_804736B0 instead of raw array indexing, implemented player/demo setup through ftDemo and Player APIs, added JObj scene setup and animation callbacks, and tightened related function prototypes in gm_1832.h. A few neighboring signatures were widened from s8/u8 to int in gm_1BA8, player, and vi0102 to support matching call ABI/codegen. No substantive review feedback was present in the provided slice.
what_changed: Implemented fn_80186EFC as a GObj/JObj animation proc that requests animation on lbl_804736B0.xC, animates the current JObj, increments a frame counter up to 0x8C, then sets a completion flag.
Introduced typed static structs for lbl_804D6618 and lbl_804736B0, replacing at least some raw lbl_804736B0 array indexing with named fields x0, x4, x8, and xC.

Implemented fn_801873F0 to initialize demo object allocation, initialize all players, configure player 0 character/costume/id/slottype/facing/model scale/state, then attach fn_80186F6C as a process to Player_GetEntity(0).
Implemented fn_80187494 as a camera/GX callback using HSD_CObjSetCurrent, Camera_800313E0, gxlink_prios assignment, HSD_GObj_80390ED0, and HSD_CObjEndCurrent.

Implemented fn_801874FC to create a GObj, load a SceneDesc joint, attach the JObj, set up GX link and proc callback, initialize model animation, hide/translate specific child JObjs by setting translate Z to 10000.0F, and cache child JObjs into lbl_804736B0.x8 and lbl_804736B0.xC.
Updated gm_8018776C_OnFrame and gm_801877A8_OnEnter to use typed lbl_804736B0 fields instead of integer array slots.
Added dont_inline pragmas around fn_801873F0 and gm_801877A8_OnEnter, indicating inlining control was needed for matching.
Implemented fn_80187C9C as a callback that disables Z mode with GXSetZMode(0, GX_NEVER, 0), calls HSD_GObj_JObjCallback, then invalidates HSD state bit 0x40.
Implemented gm_8018841C as a boolean check for gm_801A4310() == MJ_TRAINING.
Changed prototypes for gm_801BEFD0 from u8 return to int return, Player_SetCostumeId costume_id from s8 to int, and vi0102_8031CB00 costume parameters from s8 to int, with matching header updates.
smart_moves: Replacing extern s32 lbl_804736B0[] with a typed static struct made the global state self-documenting enough to express flag, counter, and cached JObj pointer roles while preserving provisional x-field naming.
Signature widening from small integer types to int in Player_SetCostumeId and vi0102_8031CB00 likely avoided unnecessary sign/zero extension artifacts and better matched PowerPC ABI argument passing.
Using #pragma dont_inline locally around matching-sensitive functions constrained compiler behavior without making broad project-wide changes.

The implementation reused existing engine/player APIs such as ftDemo_ObjAllocInit, Player_InitAllPlayers, GObj_Create, HSD_JObjLoadJoint, GObj_SetupGXLink, and HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54 rather than open-coding behavior.
The JObj child lookup pattern with lb_80011E24 and cached pointers turned magic child IDs into a repeatable scene setup idiom for this game-mode code.
decomp_lessons: For matching callbacks, prefer concrete HSD_GObj* and HSD_GObj*, int prototypes over UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS once call sites reveal the callback shape.
Static data that begins as an array can often be matched more cleanly as an anonymous or local static struct when mixed integer state and pointers occupy the same symbol.
Small integer source types in prototypes are not always match-friendly; if generated code expects full int ABI behavior, widen headers and definitions consistently.
Game-mode enter routines often require exact ordering across archive/model setup, fog/audio setup, player initialization, and proc attachment; preserving order is part of the match.
Use local PAD_STACK and dont_inline only where the diff shows codegen sensitivity; these are matching tactics, not semantic improvements.
When callbacks manipulate GX/HSD state, explicit state restore or invalidation calls such as HSD_CObjEndCurrent and HSD_StateInvalidate may be required for both correctness and matching.
naming_conventions: Function names remain address-derived for unmatched/unknown game-mode functions, e.g. fn_80186EFC, fn_801873F0, fn_80187494, fn_801874FC, fn_80187C9C.
Game-mode lifecycle functions use the existing OnEnter/OnFrame suffix convention, e.g. gm_801877A8_OnEnter and gm_8018776C_OnFrame.
Static data symbols retain linker/address labels such as lbl_804D6618, lbl_804736B0, lbl_804D6610, and lbl_804D6614.
Partially understood struct fields use offset-style placeholder names x0, x4, x8, and xC rather than speculative semantic names.
Header comments preserve address annotations such as /* 186EFC */ and /* 187C9C */ beside declarations.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inserted PAD_STACK(8) in fn_80186EFC and gm_801877A8_OnEnter, and PAD_STACK(4) in fn_80187494, indicating explicit stack-shape adjustment for matching.
Used #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop around fn_801873F0 and gm_801877A8_OnEnter to prevent compiler inlining from disturbing expected assembly.
Changed small integer signatures to int in multiple files, a common tactic to align promotion, register use, and return-value codegen with the original binary.
Converted raw global array access lbl_804736B0[0]/[1] into struct field access lbl_804736B0.x0/x4 while preserving offsets.
Kept floating constants explicit as 0.0F, 1.0f, and 10000.0F where needed in animation/model setup.
Returned HSD_GObjProc* directly from HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54 in fn_801873F0, matching the function's actual return behavior.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The context reports one issue comment, but its body was not included in the excerpt, so no human review intent can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename lbl_804736B0 fields x0/x4/x8/xC with semantic names.
Search for other Player_SetCostumeId call-site matches affected by s8 versus int parameter types.
Compare gm_1832 game-mode enter functions with other demo or single-player intro game-mode setup routines.
Look for later completion of remaining stubs in gm_1832.c such as gm_80186E30_OnEnter, fn_80186F6C, fn_80187910, fn_80187AB4, and fn_80187CF4.
